repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Cornell University Department of Human Centered Design records

origination.label: Creator
origination.corpname: Cornell University. Department of Human Centered Design

did.abstract: Collection contains event posters and postcards.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Collection contains event posters and postcards and memorabilia.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Department of Human Centered Design (HCD) was established in November of 2022. It includes faculty members from the previous departments of Design and Environmental Analysis and Fiber Science and Apparel Design. The origins of both departments can be traced back to 1925, as the Department of Household Art and the Department of Textiles and Clothing. They evolved separately and then joined together in 1969 as the Department of Design and Environmental Analysis (D+EA). They stayed together until 1985, when the Department of Textiles and Apparel split from D+EA.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Cornell University Department of Human Centered Design records, #23-29-4509. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.corpname: New York State College of Human Ecology

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes event program, bag made out of recycled plastic, notepad with colored pencils, t-shirt, sewing kit and pin.
